definition: FX volatility exhibits a non-linear threshold at ~9.5% realized vol (Hansen test p<0.01) separating positive and negative carry regimes. Below threshold (n=360 months): carry +0.58%/month (t=3.87). Above threshold (n=144): carry -1.18%/month (t=-2.11). Current G10 FX vol at 7-9% places the market near the threshold.
evidence: Hansen threshold test p<0.01 for 9.5% breakpoint
Threshold has wider confidence intervals post-2015
Vol-of-vol matters more than vol level in some sub-periods
Three-zone model: below 9% (rate differentials matter), 9-12% (transitional), above 12% (rate differentials irrelevant)
Out-of-sample vol-timing Sharpe degrades from 0.2-0.4 to 0.05-0.15 after transaction costs (statistically insignificant)
By the time realized vol reaches 9.5%, initial carry drawdown has already occurred
open_questions: Is the 9.5% threshold stable across sub-samples and regimes?
Is conditioning on vol partly tautological since carry drawdowns mechanically produce volatility?
Can vol signals arrive early enough to be actionable before most drawdown damage occurs?
Whether monitoring proximity adds information beyond tracking positioning and cross-currency basis
Causal direction requires instrumental variable approach not yet attempted
